message:
feat(fr-command): honour Redis 7+ \`#!lua flags=no-writes\` shebang on EVAL/EVALSHA (br-frankenredis-r75v)

Upstream scripting_engine/script.c parses the first line of an EVAL
script when it starts with \`#!lua\` and treats the \`flags=...\`
csv as a script-level capability declaration. The most enforced
flag is \`no-writes\`: a script with that flag rejects every
write-side redis.call regardless of whether the call site is plain
EVAL or EVAL_RO.

Today our runtime ignored the shebang entirely:
  * The Lua interpreter rejected \`#!\` as a syntax error
  * EVAL+\`#!lua flags=no-writes\` ran with full write capability

Two changes:

1. fr-command::eval_cmd / evalsha_cmd: parse the shebang via new
   \`script_shebang_has_no_writes_flag\`. When set, force
   \`store.script_read_only = true\` for the duration of the call,
   so all the existing read-only-script gating downstream of
   \`store.script_read_only\` (read_only_script_write_command_error)
   triggers for EVAL too.

2. fr-command::lua_eval::eval_script: strip a leading shebang line
   before parsing — replace it with same-length whitespace so
   reported line numbers stay aligned with the user's script.

Three new tests pin the upstream behaviour:
  * \`eval_with_shebang_no_writes_flag_rejects_writes\`
  * \`eval_with_shebang_other_flags_does_not_force_read_only\`
  * \`eval_with_shebang_combined_flags_honours_no_writes\`

The other upstream flags (allow-oom / allow-stale /
allow-cross-slot-keys / no-cluster) gate paths our single-node
runtime doesn't model and remain no-ops; tracked under r75v as
follow-ups.

fr-command 652 passed; fr-conformance 183 passed; cargo clippy
\`--tests -D warnings\` clean; cargo fmt \`--check\` clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r75v (Lua scripting parity audit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
